evals.prompt: Explain what happens when you run `git rebase -i HEAD~3` and why someone would use it. Use contract mode.
evals.expected_output: A clear, natural-sounding explanation using contractions (you're, it's, don't, gonna, etc.) and informal reductions. Should feel human and casual but still technically accurate. No caveman fragments — full sentences throughout.
assertions: Response uses contractions (e.g. you're, it's, don't, can't, won't)
Response uses at least one informal reduction (gonna, wanna, kinda, etc.)
Response uses full sentences — no dropped articles or caveman fragments
Technical content about interactive rebase is accurate and complete

evals.prompt: I'm getting a memory leak in my Node.js service. The heap keeps growing over 24 hours until the process crashes. Explain the most common causes and how to debug it. Use full caveman mode.
evals.expected_output: Classic caveman style: articles dropped, fragments OK, short synonyms, contractions used throughout. Dense but accurate. Should cover event listener leaks, closure references, caching without eviction, global accumulation. Debug steps: heap snapshot, --inspect flag, clinic.js or node --trace-gc.
assertions: Articles (a/an/the) are largely absent from prose
Response uses contractions (don't, can't, it's, etc.)
Response uses short synonyms or fragment patterns typical of caveman mode
Covers at least 2 common causes of Node.js memory leaks
Mentions at least one concrete debugging tool or technique

evals.expected_output: Terse one-line comments per finding. Should flag: user can be null before .profile.email access (bug), deleteUser has no auth check or soft-delete consideration (risk), console.log in production code (nit). Format: L<N>: [severity] problem. fix.
assertions: Comments use the L<line>: format with file reference where applicable
The null-access bug on user.profile.email is flagged
At least one severity prefix (🔴/🟡/🔵/❓) is used
No throat-clearing phrases like 'I noticed that' or 'you might want to consider'
Each comment is a single line or very tightly formatted

evals.prompt: What's the difference between `Promise.all` and `Promise.allSettled` in JavaScript? Switch to ultra mode.
evals.expected_output: Ultra-compressed: prose words abbreviated where possible (fn, req, etc.), arrows for causality (X→Y), single words where sufficient. Contractions still present. Should cover: all = fail-fast on first rejection; allSettled = wait all, return status+value/reason for each. No fragments that lose technical accuracy.
assertions: Response is noticeably more compressed than full mode would produce
The fail-fast behavior of Promise.all is explained correctly
The status/value/reason shape of allSettled results is mentioned
